title: Millionaire's shortbread
subtitle: Three layers: shortbread, caramel, and dark chocolate
excerpt: A shortbread base pressed into a tin and baked, topped with a stirred condensed milk caramel, then finished with a layer of dark chocolate. All three layers need to be fully cold before cutting.
sourceNotes: Millionaire's shortbread is a twentieth-century Scottish traybake. The combination of shortbread, condensed milk caramel, and chocolate is documented in Scottish Women's Rural Institute recipe collections from the 1970s and appears in British baking magazines through the 1980s. The condensed milk caramel layer distinguishes it from earlier layered shortbreads; condensed milk became a household staple in Britain after the Second World War.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze cut squares wrapped in paper. Defrost at room temperature for 2 hours.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keep in the fridge for up to a week. Bring to room temperature for 20 minutes before eating; cold caramel can be very hard.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, for the shortbread base

content.text: Each layer must be completely set before the next goes on. If the shortbread is still warm when the caramel is poured, the caramel will seep in and the base will go soft. If the caramel is still warm when the chocolate is poured, the chocolate will melt unevenly. The 2-hour chilling time is the minimum; overnight in the fridge gives a firmer cut. Cutting millionaire's shortbread is easiest with a sharp knife warmed briefly in hot water and dried.

Heat the oven to 160°C fan. Line a 20 cm square tin with baking paper, leaving overhang on two sides to help lift out. Beat the butter and caster sugar together until combined. Add the flour and salt and mix to a soft dough. Press evenly into the base of the tin and prick all over with a fork. Bake for 20 to 22 minutes, until pale gold and just firm. Leave to cool completely in the tin.

Put the condensed milk, butter, brown sugar, and golden syrup into a medium saucepan over medium-low heat. Stir constantly as the mixture heats; it will bubble and thicken. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring throughout, until the caramel is thick, deep gold, and pulls away from the sides of the pan slightly when stirred. Pour immediately over the cold shortbread base and smooth to the edges. Leave to cool at room temperature until fully set, about 1 hour, then refrigerate.

Melt the dark chocolate and butter together in a bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water, stirring until smooth. Pour over the set caramel layer and tilt the tin to spread to the edges. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour until the chocolate is fully set. Lift the slab out using the paper overhang and cut into squares on a board.
